source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure Fine-Grained Access Control is implemented

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure Fine-Grained Access Control is implemented
input_row.description: Fine-Grained Access Control (FGAC) on Amazon DynamoDB allows you to control access to data at the row level. Using IAM policies, you can restrict access based on the content within the request. Here is how you can implement FGAC:
input_row.rationale: Fine-Grained access control helps users to create and allow specific permission within that DB.
input_row.audit: 1. Create an IAM Role • Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. • In the navigation pane, choose Roles and select Create role. • Choose AWS service as the type of trusted entity. • Choose DynamoDB as the service that will use this role, then click Next: Permissions. • On the Attach permissions policies page, choose Next: Tags. You do not need to attach a policy to this role yet. • On the Add tags page, choose Next: Review. • On the Review page, for Role name, enter a name for your role, such as DynamoDBFineGrainedAccessRole. • Choose Create role. 2. Create an IAM Policy for Fine-Grained Access Control • In the navigation pane, choose Policies and select Create policy. • Choose the JSON tab. • Paste the following policy into the policy document field, replacing us-west-2 , 123456789012 , myddbtable , HK , and RANGEK with your own values:  { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "dynamodb:GetItem", "dynamodb:BatchGetItem", "dynamodb:Query", "dynamodb:PutItem", "dynamodb:UpdateItem", "dynamodb:DeleteItem" ], "Resource": "arn:aws:dynamodb:<us-west- 2:123456789012:table/myddbtable>", "Condition": { "ForAllValues:StringEquals": { "dynamodb:LeadingKeys": ["${www.amazon.com:user_id}"], "dynamodb:Attributes": [ "<HK>", "<RANGEK>" ] }, "StringEqualsIfExists": { "dynamodb:Select": "SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES" } } } ] } In this policy: • dynamodb:LeadingKeys restrict access to only the items where the hash key value is the same as the user's ID. • dynamodb:Attributes restrict access to only the "HK" and "RANGEK" attributes of the items. • dynamodb:Select only allows the SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES operator. • Choose Next: Tags, add any tags if needed, and then choose Next: Review. • For Name, enter a name for your policy, such as DynamoDBFineGrainedAccessPolicy. • Choose Create policy. 3. Attach the Policy to the Role • In the navigation pane, choose Roles. • Choose the role that you created in the previous step. • On the Permissions tab, choose Attach policies. • In the Filter policies search box, enter the policy name you created before. • Select the check box for your policy, then choose Attach policy.  Note : Fine-grained access control is a powerful feature but can be complex to configure. Be sure to test your setup to ensure it works as expected thoroughly.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_dynamodb_table_fine_grained_access_control_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To verify that Fine-Grained Access Control (FGAC) is implemented for Amazon DynamoDB, the approach would involve checking IAM policies associated with roles or users that interact with DynamoDB tables. The automation would need to assess the specific permissions granted and ensure they include the required conditions for FGAC as specified:

1. **Enumerate IAM Roles and Policies:**
   - Use the AWS IAM API with `boto3` to list roles using `iam.list_roles()` and list policies using `iam.list_policies()`.

2. **Identify Relevant Roles/Policies:**
   - For each policy, retrieve the policy document using `iam.get_policy()` and then `iam.get_policy_version()` to fetch the current version's document.
   - Search through the policies for permissions related to `dynamodb:GetItem`, `dynamodb:BatchGetItem`, `dynamodb:Query`, `dynamodb:PutItem`, `dynamodb:UpdateItem`, and `dynamodb:DeleteItem`.

3. **Check FGAC Conditions:**
   - Examine the condition block of each policy statement to ensure it includes the following:
     - `ForAllValues:StringEquals` for `dynamodb:LeadingKeys` with a dynamic reference like `${www.amazon.com:user_id}`
     - `StringEqualsIfExists` for `dynamodb:Select` with value `SPECIFIC_ATTRIBUTES`
   - Confirm these conditions are combined with actions applied to the resources under the Admin Control Conditions section.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If there are policies in place ensuring FGAC with the correct conditions (as per specified format and constraints).
   - **Fail:** If no such policies exist or if the conditions are invalid/incorrect.

This automated process leverages the power of AWS IAM APIs to determine if the configuration aligns with the FGAC requirements, ensuring compliance with the specified control.
